heading: Shoalhaven Heads vs Shooters Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Shoalhaven Heads, New South Wales and Shooters Hill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Shooters Hill has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Shoalhaven Heads (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Shoalhaven Heads or Shooters Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Shoalhaven Heads has a flood risk score of 27/100, while Shooters Hill scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Shoalhaven Heads scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Shooters Hill scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Shoalhaven Heads or Shooters Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Shooters Hill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
